---
title: "Prerequisites"
---

To make the most of the hackathon, please complete these steps before we begin at 14:00 on Monday 18th May.

## 1. Create a GitHub Account
If you don't have one already, sign up at [github.com](https://github.com). You will use this to share your code and presentations.

## 2. Software installation

We recommend one of the following setups:

### Option A: Local R Environment (Recommended for spatial analysis)
- **R**: [Download R](https://cran.r-project.org/)
- **RStudio**: [Download RStudio Desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)
- **Key Packages**: Run the following in your R console:

  ```r
  install.packages(c("sf", "tidyverse", "tmap", "stplanr", "od"))
  ```

### Option B: Local Python Environment
- **Python 3.10+**
- **Key Libraries**:
  ```bash
  pip install geopandas pandas matplotlib leafmap osmnx
  ```

### Option C: Cloud-Based (No installation required)
- **[GitHub Codespaces](https://github.com/codespaces/new?repo=pctni/hack)**: We will provide a template repository that you can launch in your browser. Note: Using Codespaces requires a **[GitHub account](https://github.com/signup)**.
- **Posit Cloud**: Great for R users who don't want to install software locally.

### Option D: Other Tools

Feel free to use any tools you are comfortable with (e.g., QGIS, Tableau, JavaScript libraries). Just make sure you can share your work in a reproducible way.

## 3. Data Access
You can preview and download the core datasets from the [PCTNI project repository](https://github.com/george-oakman/pctni).

Specifically, try downloading:
- [zones_sdz.gpkg](https://github.com/george-oakman/pctni/raw/refs/heads/main/pctni/zones_sdz.gpkg)
- [od_ni_open_filtered.csv](https://github.com/george-oakman/pctni/raw/refs/heads/main/pctni/od_ni_open_filtered.csv)

## 4. Familiarise Yourself
Take a look at the [Propensity to Cycle Tool (PCT)](https://www.pct.bike/) and the [PCTNI project](https://github.com/george-oakman/pctni) to understand the types of questions we are trying to answer.
